title: ferula bucharica
intro: Ferula bucharica, also known as Bucharica or Dovyan, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Central Asia, where it grows up to 1.5 meters in height. The plant's resin, which contains ferulic acid and other compounds, has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Ferula bucharica can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections.
